summary: McKenzie reports the results of running a Bits du Jour promotional deal for Bingo Card Creator, getting exactly the 2 discounted sales he expected from the promotion. He also had a record 5 regular-price sales the same day, suspecting some customers may have downloaded from Bits du Jour but purchased through the app without using the discount. He debates whether to credit those customers with the promotional price and notes the promotion drove about 60 visits total.
key_points: The Bits du Jour promotion yielded 2 sales at the discounted price, matching expectations for a niche product
5 additional full-price sales on the same day suggest some promotional traffic converted without using the discount
Attribution challenges arise when customers enter through one channel but convert through another
About 60 visits from Bits du Jour indicates the platform has meaningful traffic for less niche products
money_quotes: End result of the Bits du Jour promotion: 2 sales, which was exactly what I had been expecting.
Ahh well, I am a wee bit niche, after all.
I also had 5 sales from "regular" customers on the same day, which is an all time record.
For those of you who sell a somewhat less niche product, Bits du Jour could potentially make you a nice bit of money.
key_insight: Promotional platforms can drive sales even for niche products, but attribution gaps between discovery channel and purchase channel can obscure the true impact of a promotion.
